review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the NIST AC-6(7) control, focusing on the review of user privileges in Kubernetes through RBAC. The naming conventions mostly follow the prescribed pattern, but one function required a slight modification for clarity and specificity. The functions map correctly to Kubernetes security controls related to RBAC.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly specify the aspect of the review, such as 'least_privilege', to enhance clarity.
title: Least Privilege - Least privilege | review of user privileges (a) review [ assignment
step1_reasoning: The control AC-6(7) focuses on the review of user privileges to ensure least privilege is maintained. In Kubernetes, this maps to ensuring that RBAC configurations are regularly reviewed to prevent excessive permissions. This includes checking for the use of cluster-admin roles and ensuring that role bindings are appropriate and do not grant unnecessary privileges.
